description: A static screenshot was treated as proof even though changed interactions were never exercised.
body: Failure mode: the page loads and a screenshot looks plausible, but the changed menu, dialog, form, hover state, keyboard path, or error state is broken. Diagnostic trigger: validation mentions only first paint or visual inspection while the task changed interactive behavior. Do-not-use signal: do not claim browser validation passed until the changed interaction and at least one relevant alternate state were exercised. Recovery: rerun browser smoke with explicit steps, collect console/network output after interaction, and record the exact state that remains untested if automation is blocked. Risk boundary: this is especially dangerous for controls that submit data or hide destructive actions.
